intro.description: Configure, plan missions, and fly any ArduPilot, PX4, or Betaflight drone. Pair with ADOS Drone Agent to run intelligence at the edge. Open source. Runs in your browser.
intro.privacy: Your data stays on this device.
intro.privacyDetail: We don't collect or track anything.

disclaimer.title: Before You Fly
disclaimer.subtitle: ADOS Mission Control can control real aircraft. Please read and accept the following.

lawfulUse.heading: Lawful Use Only
lawfulUse.body: You agree to use this software only for lawful purposes. You will not use it to conduct surveillance, harass individuals, violate privacy, or support any activity prohibited by law in your country or region.

militaryUse.heading: No Unauthorized Military Use
militaryUse.body: This software must not be used for offensive military or defense operations without explicit authorization from the relevant government authority. Unauthorized weaponization of drones is illegal in virtually every jurisdiction.

aviationRegs.heading: Aviation Regulations
aviationRegs.body: You are solely responsible for complying with all aviation regulations that apply to your location and operation. This includes, but is not limited to:
aviationRegs.item1: Registering your drone with the appropriate authority (DGCA, FAA, CASA, EASA, etc.)
aviationRegs.item2: Obtaining required pilot certifications or licenses
aviationRegs.item4: Operating within altitude, distance, and visual-line-of-sight limits
aviationRegs.footer: This software does not guarantee regulatory compliance. It is a tool. You are the pilot in command.

exportControls.heading: Export Controls
exportControls.body: Drone control software may be subject to export control laws including ITAR (US), EAR (US), EU Dual-Use Regulation, and India's SCOMET list. You are responsible for ensuring that your use, distribution, or modification of this software complies with applicable export control and sanctions laws.

noWarranty.heading: No Warranty & Assumption of Risk
noWarranty.warranty: This software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind, express or implied. This includes, but is not limited to, warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.
noWarranty.risk: Drones are physical machines that can cause property damage, injury, or death if operated improperly or if software malfunctions. By using this software, you assume all risk associated with its use. Altnautica and its contributors shall not be liable for any claim, damage, or loss arising from the use of this software, whether direct, indirect, incidental, or consequential.

openSource.heading: Open Source License
openSource.body: ADOS Mission Control is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0. You are free to use, modify, and distribute this software under the terms of that license.

disclaimer.acceptCheckbox: I have read and accept the terms above. I understand that I am the pilot in command and assume all responsibility for safe and lawful operation.

preferences.title: Set your preferences
preferences.jurisdictionOptional: Optional — sets airspace rules and default units
preferences.demoModeDescription: Try the interface with 5 simulated drones. Turn off when you connect real hardware.
preferences.audioAlertsDescription: Battery, GPS loss, and failsafe warnings with sound.

theme.title: Choose your theme
theme.subtitle: You can change this anytime in settings
theme.tapToSwap: Tap any tile to swap with preview

workspace.missionStatusSample: 5 waypoints loaded. Ready to arm and start mission.

download.title: Desktop App Available
download.description: The desktop app works offline with zero external dependencies. Your data stays on your machine, nothing phones home. Desktop releases follow a weekly update cycle and may trail the web version by a release or two.

ready.title: You're good to go.
ready.communityDescription: ADOS Mission Control is free, open-source, and community-driven. We build this in public and every contribution matters.
ready.discordDescription: Ask questions, share your setup, report bugs, or just hang out with other pilots and developers.
ready.githubDescription: A star on GitHub helps more people find this project. It takes two seconds and makes a real difference.
ready.docsDescription: Guides, API reference, and tutorials to get your drone flying.

region.title: Operating region
region.subtitle: How your drones handle radio regulations. You can change this any time, per drone.
region.optionUnrestrictedHint: Radio works out of the box anywhere. You are responsible for legal RF operation in your jurisdiction.
region.optionOtherHint: Enter a two-letter ISO country code.
region.unrestrictedNote: Operator responsible for local RF compliance.
region.perDroneNote: This is the default applied when a drone is paired. Each drone can be set individually on its System tab.

localStorageBanner.warning: Your data is saved in this browser only. Clearing browser data will erase it.

unsavedChangesDialog.title: Unsaved Changes
unsavedChangesDialog.message: You have unsaved changes to the current plan. What would you like to do?

auth.name: Full name

auth.signInSyncDescription: Sign in to sync your missions across devices.
auth.createAccountBackupDescription: Create an account to back up your data to the cloud.
auth.alreadyHaveAccountSignIn: Already have an account? Sign in

errors.noAccountFound: No account found with this email.

vision.listFailed: Could not load models from the agent
vision.emptyRegistry: No models in the registry yet.

vision.overlayHint: Bounding boxes draw over the live video here and on the main flight view.

dronePlugins.subtitle: Extensions installed on this drone. Each plugin runs sandboxed and only does what its granted permissions allow.
dronePlugins.emptyInstalledHint: Nothing installed yet. Browse available extensions below, or upload a .adosplug file.
dronePlugins.emptyStateTitle: No plugins installed on this drone yet.
dronePlugins.emptyStateBody: Plugins extend ADOS with vendor cameras, gimbals, custom telemetry, vision algorithms, and more. Each runs only on the drone you install it to.
dronePlugins.notConnectedTitle: Connect to a drone to manage its plugins.
dronePlugins.notConnectedCloud: Cloud relay is awaiting a heartbeat from the agent.
dronePlugins.notConnectedLocal: Open a paired node from the sidebar to begin.
dronePlugins.uninstallConfirmMessage: Remove {name} from this drone? Plugin data on this drone will be cleared.
dronePlugins.permissionGrantPartialFailure: Plugin installed, but {count} permission grant(s) failed: {permissions}. Open the plugin detail page to retry.
dronePlugins.agentNotConnected: Agent not connected. Connect this drone before installing a plugin.
dronePlugins.demoInstallDisabled: Plugin install is disabled in demo mode.
dronePlugins.logsPending: Plugin logs view will land in the next release.
dronePlugins.permissionsLinkPending: Permissions detail page will land in the next release.

droneRadio.rssiAirNote: Drone does not receive its own RF; ground station reports the RSSI value.
droneRadio.txPowerScopeHint: Adjusts air-side TX power on this drone.
droneRadio.noAgent: No agent connection. TX power changes are unavailable until the drone reconnects.

pairing.enterCodeTitle: Pair with a code from your drone
pairing.enterCodeSubtitle: Works over HTTPS. No LAN access required.
pairing.enterCodeHint: Run `ados status` on your drone to see its current code, or copy it from the install banner.

command.enterPairingCode: Enter pairing code from your node agent
command.pairingInstructions: Run 'ados pair' on your node to get a pairing code
command.selectNode: Select a node from the sidebar to connect
command.pairToStart: Pair a node to get started
command.firstTime: First time? Run this on your node
command.firstTimeColon: First time? Run this on your node:
command.waitingForNode: Waiting for your node to connect...
command.codeExpiredMessage: The pairing code timed out after 15 minutes.
command.reconnectTooltip: Re-subscribe to cloud heartbeats for this node
command.cloudRelayTimeout: This node hasn't sent a heartbeat via cloud relay. Open Mission Control from the LAN URL for direct mode, or sign in and pair the agent with a 6-character code to enable cloud relay.
command.agentUnreachableHint: Tried {url}. Check that the agent is powered on and on the same network. If the hostname has moved, try Re-resolve host.
command.missingLanCredentials: Missing LAN credentials. Re-pair this node from the Add-a-Node card.

addNode.title: Add a node
addNode.subtitle: Pair any ADOS node (drone, ground station, or future compute) directly over your LAN.
addNode.addNodeTitle: Add a drone or ground station
addNode.addNodeSubtitle: No account required. Works fully offline.
addNode.addNodeHint: Same Wi-Fi as the agent? Paste the hostname or IP. New agent showing a 6-character code in `ados status`? Type that.
addNode.codeTryHostnameTip: Tip: if the agent is on your network, enter its hostname or IP instead (for example my-drone.local).
addNode.codeNoHostError: Pair code is valid but the agent hasn't advertised a network address yet. Wait a few seconds and try again.
addNode.codeNoLanMatchError: No agent on this LAN is advertising that pair code. Make sure you're on the same Wi-Fi and `ados status` on the agent shows this code, or add the agent by its hostname or IP instead.{hint}
addNode.badCodeError: Pair code must be six characters (letters and digits).
addNode.pairOnLan: Pair a node on your LAN
addNode.noAccountRequired: No account required. Works fully offline.
addNode.installAgent: First time? Install a new agent
addNode.installAgentDescription: Run this on the SBC, then paste the hostname above.
addNode.signInForRemote: Reach drones from outside this network
addNode.signInDescription: Sign in to enable cloud relay.
addNode.signInBody: Cloud relay routes your drone's telemetry and video through Convex over HTTPS, so Mission Control reaches the agent even when your phone or laptop is on a different network. Optional, never blocking — LAN-direct pairing works without it.
addNode.enterHostnameError: Enter a hostname or URL to probe
addNode.probeFailedError: Could not reach that address. Check the agent is running and on the same network.
addNode.alreadyPairedMessage: This agent is already paired. Pair locally to attempt to take ownership. The agent will respond with a clear error if it can't.
addNode.alreadyPairedToOtherBrowser: This agent is already paired to another browser. Unpair from the agent's setup page, then try again.
addNode.mixedContentError: Mission Control is on HTTPS and browsers block direct HTTP fetches to LAN agents. Use the "Pair with a code" card below, or run the desktop app or a localhost build to pair over LAN.
addNode.hostNotPrivateError: Only private network hosts (RFC1918 IPs, *.local names, or loopback) are allowed for LAN pairing.
addNode.pairedButConnectFailed: Paired, but could not establish a live connection: {error}. The node is saved. Retry from the sidebar.
addNode.wipePairStateConfirm: This deletes the cloud pair record and any heartbeat history for this device. The agent itself stays paired; unpair it from the agent's setup webapp if needed. Continue?
addNode.wipePairStateSuccess: Pair state wiped. Try pairing again.

firstPairWarning.title: Your API keys live in this browser
firstPairWarning.body: Clearing browser storage will unpair every locally-paired node. To recover, unpair from the agent's setup webapp at http://your-agent.local:8080/setup.html and re-pair from here.

consolidatingNotice.title: Hardware controls are consolidating into the Command tab.
consolidatingNotice.body: The Command tab now hosts the local-first Add-a-Node flow and will host every node type (drone agent, ground station, relay, receiver, future compute) under one sidebar. Hardware will stay available during the transition for deeper sub-views.
consolidatingNotice.computeBody: The compute profile is a workstation-class node that consumes survey datasets and produces orthomosaics, NeRF reconstructions, and gaussian splats. See product/specs/compute-agent/ for the full design.

demoMode.title: Demo data is not available for ground-station controls
demoMode.body: These tabs talk to a live agent over REST. Pair a real ground station from the Add-a-Node card on the Command tab to use them.

limited.title: Cloud relay connection
limited.network: Network configuration writes through the agent's REST surface, which is only reachable on the same LAN. Connect to the agent's setup page from a device on the same network to change WiFi, Ethernet, modem, or uplink priority.
limited.display: LCD calibration and remote page control write through the agent's REST surface, which is only reachable on the same LAN. Read-only status is in the Overview card.
limited.physicalUi: OLED brightness, screen cycle, and Bluetooth pairing route through the agent's REST surface, which is only reachable on the same LAN. Connect on LAN to make changes.
limited.peripherals: Peripheral rescan and per-peripheral actions route through the agent's REST surface, which is only reachable on the same LAN. The list below comes from the cloud heartbeat and is read-only.
limited.mesh: batman-adv mesh topology (neighbours, routes, gateways) is local to the ground station and never relayed through the cloud. Connect on LAN to inspect mesh health.
limited.distributedRx: Distributed receive fragment counters and pairing controls are local to the ground station and never relayed through the cloud. Connect on LAN for the live view.

agent.shouldReportShortly: The agent should report in shortly

radioStack.label: Radio stack
radioStack.hint: The on-board radio stack is reporting a problem that is separate from pairing. Check the radio adapter, driver, and bind artifacts.

history.title: Flight Logs
history.emptySubtitle: Connect a drone and arm to start your first recording. Imported logs and live recordings will appear here.
history.deleteConfirm: Delete {count} flight record(s)? This cannot be undone.
history.logBrowserNoDrone: Connect a real flight controller to browse onboard logs.
history.logBrowserArmedRefuse: Disarm the vehicle before pulling logs — pulling while armed can corrupt the buffer.
history.logBrowserMockNotice: Mock protocol active. Connect real hardware to import dataflash logs.
history.logBrowserNoLogs: No logs on the flight controller.
history.logBrowserRcInMissing: Heads-up: this log does not include RC stick inputs. Set LOG_BITMASK to include RCIN before the next flight if you want them.
history.uploadLogDrop: Drop a .bin file here, or click to browse.
history.importDrop: Drop log files here, or click to browse
history.mediaDrop: Drop photos or videos here, or click to browse
history.mediaFormats: JPEG, PNG, WebP, HEIC, MP4, MOV, WebM
history.mediaNoMedia: No media linked to this flight. Use the Import Media button to add photos or videos.
history.chartsNoRecording: No telemetry recording attached to this flight.
history.customChartEmpty: Build a custom chart from any telemetry channel and field.
history.correlationNoOverlap: No overlapping samples. Try fields recorded at similar rates.
history.spectrogramInsufficient: Not enough vibration samples ({count}). Need ≥32 for spectrogram.
history.mapNoPath: No path data for this flight.
history.exportNoRecording: No telemetry recording attached. Connect and arm a drone to start recording.
history.exportNoPath: No path data on this flight.
history.notesSealed: This record is sealed. Unseal from the panel header to edit.
history.windMetarNote: METAR wind shown above is from the nearest station. This estimate is derived from the flight controller.

library.title: FLIGHT PLANS
library.deleteConfirm: Delete this plan? This cannot be undone.

fcShared.armedLockMessage: Configuration changes are disabled while the vehicle is armed. Disarm to make changes.
fcShared.disconnectedMessage: Connect a flight controller to access configuration panels.
fcShared.rebootRequired: Reboot required for changes to take effect
fcShared.missingParams: Some optional parameters are not available on this firmware
fcShared.pendingFlashCount: {count, plural, one {1 param} other {{count} params}} modified in RAM, not written to flash

calibration.title: Calibration
calibration.pressWhenReady: Press when vehicle is still in position
calibration.sensorCalibrationDesc: Step-by-step calibration wizards for accelerometer, gyroscope, compass, level, and airspeed
calibration.connectToCalibrate: Connect a drone to run calibrations
calibration.bfAccelDesc: Place the quad on a flat surface and keep it still. Betaflight calibration takes about 5 seconds.
calibration.bfAccelStepDesc: Keep the quad perfectly still on a flat surface
calibration.bfCalibrationNote1: Betaflight supports accelerometer calibration via MSP. Gyro calibration runs automatically on every boot. Compass calibration (if a magnetometer is present) should be done through the Betaflight Configurator or CLI.
calibration.bfCalibrationNote2: Other calibration types (level, airspeed, barometer, ESC, RC, CompassMot) are ArduPilot-specific and not available on Betaflight.
calibration.accelCalibrationDesc: 6-position calibration. Place vehicle in each orientation when prompted.
calibration.gyroCalibrationDesc: Keep vehicle perfectly still during calibration.
calibration.compassCalibrationDesc: Rotate vehicle slowly in all orientations until complete.
calibration.compassTip1: Move at least 3m away from metal objects, vehicles, and buildings
calibration.compassTip2: Remove magnetic accessories (phone mounts, metal tools) from nearby
calibration.compassTip3: Ensure GPS fix if possible (helps validate compass orientation)
calibration.compassTip4: Keep a firm grip, you will rotate the drone through all axes
calibration.compassOrientationChangedDesc: The calibration detected a different compass orientation than previously configured. Verify mounting orientation in parameters after reboot.

parameters.title: Parameters
parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.resetMessage: This will reset ALL parameters to firmware defaults. You will need to re-calibrate sensors afterward. The flight controller will require a reboot.
parameters.rebootMessage: Some parameters you changed require a flight controller reboot to take effect. Reboot now?

preArm.title: Pre-Arm Checks
preArm.gpsNoFix: GPS does not have a fix
preArm.gpsNoFixSuggestion: Wait for GPS lock or check antenna connection
preArm.compassSuggestion: Run compass calibration in the Calibrate tab
preArm.accelSuggestion: Run accelerometer calibration in the Calibrate tab
preArm.rcSuggestion: Complete RC calibration in the Calibrate tab
preArm.batterySuggestion: Charge battery or check BATT_LOW_VOLT parameter
preArm.loggingSuggestion: Check SD card or LOG_BACKEND_TYPE parameter
preArm.fenceSuggestion: Upload geofence in the Plan tab
preArm.ekfSuggestion: Wait for EKF to settle or recalibrate sensors
preArm.safetySuggestion: Press the safety switch on the flight controller
preArm.missionSuggestion: Fix mission errors in the Plan tab
preArm.hardwareSafetySuggestion: Press the physical safety button to allow arming
preArm.clickToRun: Click "Run Check" to verify pre-arm status

flightModes.circleDesc: Circle around a point of interest
flightModes.acroDesc: Full manual rate control, no leveling
flightModes.fbwaDesc: Fly by wire A - stabilized manual
flightModes.fbwbDesc: Fly by wire B - stabilized with altitude hold

actionDialogs.armMessage: Arm the motors? Ensure the area is clear and all pre-flight checks pass.
actionDialogs.disarmMessage: Disarm the motors? The vehicle must be on the ground.
actionDialogs.rthMessage: Command the vehicle to return to the launch position?
actionDialogs.abortMessage: Abort the current mission? The vehicle will hold position.
actionDialogs.killMessage: This will IMMEDIATELY CUT ALL MOTORS. The drone will fall from the sky. This is an emergency-only action. Are you absolutely sure?
actionDialogs.killFinalMessage: Motors will be cut immediately. Drone will fall.

checklist.title: Pre-Flight Checklist
checklist.allComplete: All checks complete — ready for flight

disconnectedPage.installAndConnect: Install the agent, enter this code, and you're connected.
disconnectedPage.turnAnyDrone: Turn any drone into a smart drone
disconnectedPage.mavlinkProxyDesc: Full bidirectional flight controller access over WebSocket, TCP, and UDP. Configure, monitor, and control your drone from anywhere.
disconnectedPage.hdVideoDesc: Live HD video from your drone to any browser. Low-latency streaming powered by WFB-ng and OpenHD with RTL8812-series WiFi adapters.
disconnectedPage.cellularTelemetryDesc: Fly beyond visual line of sight. Real-time telemetry and control over cellular networks. No range limits.
disconnectedPage.extendedRangeDesc: Push your control range to 50km+ using RTL8812AU/EU WiFi adapters in monitor mode. Purpose-built for long-range drone operations.
disconnectedPage.plugAndPlayDesc: Automatic hardware detection and setup. The agent recognizes your flight controller, sensors, and peripherals on first boot. No manual configuration needed.
disconnectedPage.aiReadyDesc: Built for autonomous operations. Python SDK, scripting engine, and REST API let you build intelligent flight behaviors and integrate ML models.
disconnectedPage.softwareDefinedDesc: Turn any ArduPilot or PX4 drone into a smart drone. One companion computer. Full software control over every system.
disconnectedPage.sshTerminalDesc: Full TUI dashboard over SSH. Five screens for telemetry, MAVLink inspection, config editing, and logs. No browser needed.
disconnectedPage.devToolsDesc: REST API at :8080, Python SDK, YAML missions, CLI tools, and five scripting tiers from simple text commands to full autonomy.
disconnectedPage.alphaDisclaimer: This is alpha software. Expect bugs, breaking changes, and incomplete features. The Command tab provides direct control over drone agent services and configuration. Use at your own risk.

unpair.title: Unpair drone?
unpair.body: The pair key will be regenerated and the drone will need to re-pair.

leaveWifi.title: Leave WiFi network?
leaveWifi.body: The ground node will fall back to its access-point mode.

shareUplink.title: Share uplink with WiFi clients?
shareUplink.body: This installs a NAT rule and routes connected clients' traffic over the active uplink.

factoryReset.title: Factory reset?
factoryReset.body: All ground-node configuration including any mesh identity will be erased. Type the phrase below to confirm.

meshRevokeRelay.title: Revoke relay?
meshRevokeRelay.body: Type the relay device id to confirm. The relay will stop contributing to reception.

meshChangeRole.title: Change ground-station role?
meshChangeRole.body: Changing role restarts networking. Type the phrase below to confirm.

distributedRx.pairingIdle: Closed. Open a window to accept new relays.
distributedRx.emptyDirect: This node is in direct mode. Set the role to relay or receiver to use distributed reception.
distributedRx.emptyUnset: Set the ground-station role from the device OLED to begin.

mesh.title: Mesh
mesh.notUp: Mesh is not up. Set the role to relay or receiver to bring it online.
mesh.downloadLogsHint: Aggregate logs from every mesh node into a single archive.
mesh.downloadLogsComingSoon: Backend bundle endpoint not yet available.
mesh.emptyDirect: This node is in direct mode. Set the role to relay or receiver to view the mesh.
mesh.pinHint: Pin applies on next mesh service restart. Use Auto to revert.

wifiClient.title: WiFi Client
wifiClient.noNetworks: Tap Scan to look for nearby networks.
wifiClient.forceJoinWarning: This will stop the Access Point. Continue?

shareUplink.title: Share uplink with AP clients
shareUplink.description: Routes active uplink traffic out the ground station Access Point. Advanced option.

localDisplay.calibratedTooltip: Touch panel matches the current rotation.
localDisplay.uncalibratedTooltip: Run calibration to align touches with the panel.
localDisplay.calibrateStarted: Calibration started — tap each crosshair shown on the device LCD.
localDisplay.overrideNote: Changes apply at the next display service restart.

lcdCalibration.title: Calibrate touch screen

lcdCalibration.statusFailed: Lost contact with the agent during calibration

androidRc.title: ADOS Android RC Controller
androidRc.stubBody: The ADOS Android RC handheld controller is in active development. This panel will light up when the product ships.

overviewPage.title: Overview
overviewPage.description: At-a-glance status of your ground station: who is paired, where uplink is going, and how the radio link is performing.
overviewPage.dragHint: Drag uplinks in Network to change priority
overviewPage.noAgentBody: Connect to an ADOS ground station agent to manage hardware, radios, and peripherals.

radio.title: Radio
radio.description: WFB-ng video radio link, TX power, and channel control.
radio.brownoutWarning: USB host VBUS topology browns out above 18 dBm. Slider capped at 15 dBm.
radio.confirmHighPowerTitle: Confirm TX power above safe floor
radio.confirmHighPowerBody: Type the agent hostname to confirm setting TX power to {dbm} dBm.

pairing.title: Pairing
pairing.pickPeerLocal: Both rigs share the radio. One button.
pairing.pickPeerRemote: Pick a {role} to pair this {selfRole} with:
pairing.confirmUnpairBody: Wipes both key files and clears pair state. Type "unpair" to confirm.
pairing.errorTimeout: Pairing timed out. Make sure the peer rig is powered on within radio range.
pairing.errorFingerprintMismatch: Fingerprints did not match. The link was rolled back automatically.
pairing.errorBindBusy: A bind session is already in progress on this rig.
pairing.errorRearmBlocked: Cannot re-arm auto-pair while paired. Unpair first.
pairing.armedDescription: Looking for an unpaired peer on the radio. Will pair on first contact, then disarm.

peerDiagnostics.noFrames: No frames received from peer yet

radio.notSupported: Radio control not supported on this agent

tuning.title: Link tuning
tuning.description: Tune the radio rate and error correction for this link. Changes apply live and persist across restarts.
tuning.fecHint: More redundancy survives heavier loss at lower throughput.
tuning.mcsHint: A higher index sends more data but needs a stronger signal.
tuning.mcsConfirmBody: MCS {mcs} needs a strong signal. A marginal link may drop. Apply it?
tuning.adaptiveHint: Automatically raise error correction when the link degrades.
tuning.calibrateHint: Measure this link and pick the best settings.

calibration.title: Calibrate link
calibration.intro: Sweeps transmit settings and measures the link at the receiver to recommend the best rate and error correction. Keep both ends powered and in position; the link briefly drops on each step.
calibration.noReceiver: No receiving ground station with decode stats found in the fleet. Connect a receiver and retry.
calibration.marginalWarn: No setting was fully clean. This is the best of a marginal link.
calibration.noneWorked: No setting held the link. Check antennas, distance, and channel.

radio.adapterUsbDegraded: Radio USB link degraded — RF may not transmit
radio.adapterUsbDegradedSpeed: Radio USB link degraded ({speed} Mbps) — RF may not transmit
radio.adapterUsbDegradedHint: The radio adapter enumerated on a slow full-speed USB link instead of high-speed (480 Mbps). It can still report transmit activity while emitting no usable RF. Move the adapter to a USB-3 (blue) port or a powered hub.
radio.reacquireKillsHint: How many times the ground receiver has had to destructively restart to recover a valid decode. A high or climbing value means the receive link is thrashing.
radio.rxZombieKillsHint: How many times the ground receiver was alive yet silently stopped decoding, so the liveness watchdog had to restart it. A climbing value means the receive process keeps wedging without exiting.
radio.radioStackHint: The agent's coarse rollup of the radio stack: OK when it is transmitting, otherwise the reason it is not (no injection-capable adapter, unpaired, missing bind keys, or an incomplete radio stack).

videoLink.title: Video Link
videoLink.acquiringDetail: The radio is searching for the peer. Video starts once the link locks.

radio.bindWaiting: Waiting for peer on the radio…

plugins.revokeConfirmMessage: The plugin will lose access to {permission}. Continue?

browse.title: Browse plugins
browse.subtitle: Discover signed extensions for the ADOS platform.
browse.emptyState: No matching plugins. Try clearing filters.
browse.installPlaceholder: Install flow connects to the local plugin host on next ship.
browse.author: Author

notCompatible.board: Not compatible with this drone's board
notCompatible.noAgent: Connect to a drone to install plugins

empty.title: No plugins available right now.
empty.subtitle: Check back later for new extensions.

tooltipReason.major_bump: Major version bump (v{current} to v{latest}) requires review
tooltipReason.board_mismatch: Not compatible with this drone's board

settings.title: Update settings: {pluginName}
settings.autoUpdateHint: Edit via the ados plugin auto-update CLI on the drone.

dialog.closingDisabled: Closing is disabled while install is in progress.

commandFleet.title: Agent Overview
commandFleet.subtitle: Live feeds and quick telemetry for every paired Drone Agent.
commandFleet.noAgentsBody: Pair a Drone Agent to see fleet video and telemetry here.

ados.subtitle: Install the ADOS agent on a companion computer

checklist.intro: Browser flash erases the board's storage. Install commands replace the agent binary on the existing OS.
checklist.dataLoss: Data on the board's storage will be erased
checklist.usbPower: Board is powered via USB only (no external supply)
checklist.backup: I have backed up any user data on the board

webusbWarning.title: WebUSB not available
webusbWarning.body: Browser flashing for this board requires WebUSB. Open Mission Control in Chrome, Edge, Brave, or Opera. Pick a different board if you only need the install command path.

pathChoice.summary: Browser flash vs install command — which do I need?
pathChoice.browserFlash: Browser flash writes a complete operating system image directly to the board's storage over USB-C. Use this when the board has no operating system yet, or when its stock OS cannot install our agent on its own. Today this is the path for the Luckfox Pico Zero.
pathChoice.installCommand: Install command is a single shell line you copy onto a board that is already booted into its stock vendor operating system (Raspberry Pi OS, Radxa OS, Ubuntu for Tegra, etc.). The installer drops the agent binary, sets up the systemd unit, and starts it. Use this when you can already SSH into the board or open a terminal on it.
pathChoice.pickerHint: The picker below selects the correct flow automatically based on the board you choose.

bootrom.summary: Putting your Luckfox into bootrom mode
bootrom.step2: Locate the BOOT button on the board (small tactile switch near the USB-C port).
bootrom.step3: Hold BOOT while plugging USB-C into your computer.
bootrom.step5: The board enumerates as a Rockchip USB device. Authorize it when the browser prompts you.
bootrom.hint: If the board does not appear, unplug it, hold BOOT a moment longer, and try again. Some USB-C cables only carry power — use a known-good data cable.

targetBoard.title: Target Board
targetBoard.offlineCatalogTooltip: The upstream release catalog was unreachable. Showing the embedded baseline. Image URLs may be missing or stale.

confirm.title: Confirm flash
confirm.body: About to flash {board}. This erases the board's storage.

curl.title: Install on a board running stock OS
curl.setupHintBefore: Open the board's setup page at
curl.setupHintAfter: after the installer finishes. Pair the device from the Fleet view in Mission Control.

webFlash.title: Flash via browser
webFlash.noImage: No image URL published yet for this board. Check the agent repo's releases.
webFlash.devTitle: Browser flash is in active development
webFlash.devBody: The in-browser Rockchip bootrom flasher is being built into Mission Control. Once it lands, plug your board into USB-C in bootrom mode and click Flash to write the eMMC from this page directly.
webFlash.checklistHint: Confirm the safety checklist above to enable the Flash button.

error.noBoardSelected: No board selected. Hold BOOT, plug USB-C, then try again.
error.noReadableBody: Image response had no readable body.
error.missingSignature: Manifest is missing a minisign signature for this image. Refusing to flash an unsigned artifact.
error.webusbBlocked: WebUSB blocked. Serve Mission Control over HTTPS or localhost.
error.usbDisconnected: USB device disconnected during flash. Hold the BOOT button while plugging USB-C in to re-enter bootrom mode, then retry.

toast.noImageUrl: No image URL available for this board.
toast.connectBoardFirst: Connect the board in bootrom mode first.
toast.flashComplete: Flash complete. Unplug and re-plug the board.

connection.scanHint: Hold the BOOT button while plugging USB-C into your computer. The board enumerates as a Rockchip USB device (vendor {vendor}). Click Scan to authorize it.

apPeriph.title: AP_Periph (CAN nodes)
apPeriph.subtitle: Flash AP_Periph firmware to DroneCAN peripheral nodes

connection.title: Connection

transport.slcanRequiresUsb: SLCAN requires a direct USB connection to the FC
transport.canForwardWaiting: Waiting for agent CAN bus capability
transport.canForwardReady: Route CAN frames through the connected agent

connection.slcanPendingUseCanForward: SLCAN flashing coming next; use CAN_FORWARD for now.

target.title: Target node
target.noNodes: No DroneCAN nodes detected. Enter SLCAN mode first.
target.noNodesActive: No DroneCAN nodes detected on the bus yet.

checklist.props: Props removed from any prop-driving nodes
checklist.rebootUnderstood: I understand the node will reboot mid-flash

postFlash.title: Post-flash actions
postFlash.subtitle: Optional follow-up actions on the freshly flashed node.

flashProgress.waitingHint: The flight controller is rebooting into its bootloader. This usually takes a few seconds.

preflight.title: Before you flash
preflight.unsupported: Firmware flashing needs Web Serial or WebUSB. Use a Chromium-based browser such as Chrome, Edge, or Brave.
preflight.insecure: Web Serial and WebUSB are only available on a secure origin. Open this page over HTTPS or on localhost.

deviceDisconnected.title: The board reconnected as its bootloader
deviceDisconnected.step1: Your flight controller rebooted into its bootloader and re-appeared as a new USB device.
deviceDisconnected.step2: Click Select device and choose the board in the picker, then flashing resumes.

syncTimeout.title: Could not reach the bootloader
syncTimeout.step1: Use a data USB cable (not a charge-only cable) plugged directly into your computer.
syncTimeout.step2: Put the board in bootloader mode: hold BOOT, plug in USB, release BOOT.

boardIdMismatch.title: Firmware does not match this board
boardIdMismatch.step1: Pick the firmware built for your exact board, then flash again.

crcMismatch.title: Verification failed
crcMismatch.step1: The written firmware did not match. Power and cable quality are the usual cause.
crcMismatch.step2: Retry on a short, good-quality USB cable with stable power.

webusbBlocked.title: Browser blocked USB access
webusbBlocked.step1: Open this page over HTTPS or on localhost so the browser can access the device.

noDevice.title: No device selected
noDevice.step1: Put the board in bootloader mode (hold BOOT, plug in USB), then retry and pick it.

browserUnsupported.title: Browser not supported
browserUnsupported.step1: Use a Chromium-based browser such as Chrome, Edge, or Brave.

unknown.title: Flash failed
unknown.step1: Check the debug log below, then retry. Download the log to share it for support.

manualBootloader.step2: Hold the BOOT button (sometimes labelled BT).
manualBootloader.step3: While holding BOOT, plug in USB, then release after 1-2 seconds.
manualBootloader.step4: Click Select device and pick the board from the picker.

wizard.noDroneHint: No drone connected. You can still flash: select your board and firmware, then connect the device when prompted.
wizard.doneHint: Reconnect the flight controller to continue, then restore your parameters if needed.

mesh.title: Mesh
mesh.notCapable: Mesh not enabled on this node.

panel.title: Vision Navigation
panel.subtitle: GPS-denied flight via optical flow and visual inertial odometry

sourceSet.label: EKF Source Set
sourceSet.switchConfirm: Switch to {{name}}? This will replan the EKF source for in-flight navigation.
sourceSet.px4NotSupported: PX4 does not support runtime source switching. Update parameters and restart the EKF.

preArm.companionBlocker: Vision companion process is {{state}}. Vision navigation cannot arm.
preArm.originBlocker: EKF origin not set. Plugin will auto-set via SET_GPS_GLOBAL_ORIGIN, retry in a few seconds.
preArm.waitingHeartbeat: Vision navigation: waiting for companion heartbeat

calibration.title: Camera + IMU Calibration
calibration.noFiles: No calibration files yet — upload Kalibr output.

placeholder.rpcTrace: RPC Trace lands in next release
placeholder.nodeStatusTimeline: Node Status Timeline lands in next release
placeholder.busHealthGauges: Bus Health Gauges land in next release

canConfig.title: CAN Configuration
canConfig.subtitle: Configure CAN bus drivers, browse DroneCAN nodes, edit node parameters, and inspect bus traffic.
canConfig.noDrone: No drone selected. Connect a flight controller to read or write CAN parameters.

busSetup.title: Bus setup
busSetup.enterSlcanConfirm: Entering SLCAN mode will pause MAVLink on the selected serial port and forward raw CAN traffic instead. Reboot the FC after save to apply.
busSetup.rebootFailed: Reboot command failed, retry or power-cycle
busSetup.notConnected: Connect a drone to read or write CAN parameters.
busSetup.loading: Loading parameters from the flight controller...

CAN_P1_DRIVER.label: Driver index
CAN_P1_DRIVER.description: Logical CAN driver this port binds to. 0 disables the port. Reboot required.

CAN_P1_BITRATE.label: Bitrate
CAN_P1_BITRATE.description: Classic CAN bitrate in bit/s. Typical DroneCAN networks run at 1 Mbit/s.

CAN_P1_FDBITRATE.label: CAN-FD bitrate
CAN_P1_FDBITRATE.description: Fast-data-phase bitrate when CAN-FD is used. Ignored on CAN 2.0.

CAN_P1_OPTIONS.label: Bus options
CAN_P1_OPTIONS.description: Bitmask for advanced CAN driver options. 0 = default.

CAN_D1_PROTOCOL.label: Protocol
CAN_D1_PROTOCOL.description: Driver-side protocol bound to CAN1. Select DroneCAN for UAVCAN nodes.

CAN_D1_UC_NODE.label: DroneCAN node ID
CAN_D1_UC_NODE.description: Local node ID used by the flight controller on this bus. Range 1 to 125.

CAN_D1_UC_OPTION.label: DroneCAN options
CAN_D1_UC_OPTION.description: Bitmask of DroneCAN driver options (e.g. enable internal filter).

CAN_P2_DRIVER.label: Driver index
CAN_P2_DRIVER.description: Logical CAN driver this port binds to. 0 disables the port. Reboot required.

CAN_P2_FDBITRATE.label: CAN-FD bitrate
CAN_P2_FDBITRATE.description: Fast-data-phase bitrate when CAN-FD is used.

CAN_D2_UC_NODE.label: DroneCAN node ID
CAN_D2_UC_NODE.description: Local node ID used by the flight controller on this bus. Range 1 to 125.

CAN_SLCAN_CPORT.label: SLCAN CAN port
CAN_SLCAN_CPORT.description: Which CAN bus is forwarded over SLCAN. 0 = disabled. Reboot required.

CAN_SLCAN_SERNUM.label: SLCAN serial port
CAN_SLCAN_SERNUM.description: Which serial port carries the SLCAN stream. Serial0 = USB is the typical choice.

CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT.label: SLCAN timeout
CAN_SLCAN_TIMOUT.description: Seconds before the SLCAN session auto-closes. 0 = no auto-close.

CAN_SLCAN_OVRIDE.label: SLCAN override
CAN_SLCAN_OVRIDE.description: When on, SLCAN takes ownership of the bus even if the FC was using it.

testUtilities.title: Test utilities
testUtilities.subtitle: Bench tools for bringing up new DroneCAN nodes and reproducing field reports.

conflictScan.title: Node-ID conflict scanner
conflictScan.conflictDetected: Result: {count} node(s) claiming ID {first} with different UIDs

escSweep.title: ESC RawCommand sweep
escSweep.safetyMessage: ESC RawCommand drives motor output directly. Confirm props are removed and the motors are bolted to a bench fixture before continuing.

no-connection.title: No drones connected
no-connection.body: Plug a flight controller into USB or your network to fly it directly, or pair a companion computer to add video, scripts and long range links.

locked.title: Add a companion computer to unlock {surface}
locked.body: This drone is connected by flight controller only. Pair a companion computer, a small board like a Raspberry Pi running the ADOS agent, to stream HD video, run scripts, add plugins and reach the drone over 4G.

no-fc-direct.title: Flight controller not connected
no-fc-direct.body: Connect a flight controller to read live data from this drone. The parameter tree, calibration and configuration load once it is talking. Nothing is written until you connect.

no-fc-agent.title: No flight controller detected by the agent
no-fc-agent.body: The companion computer is online but sees no autopilot on its serial ports. Check the flight controller is powered and wired to the board.

fc-unverified.title: Flight controller link not verified
fc-unverified.body: The agent reports {port} at {baud} but has not heard from the autopilot yet. It may be booting or set to a different baud rate.

agent-offline.title: Agent offline, no heartbeat
agent-offline.body: Last update {ago}. The readings shown are the last the agent reported. Power or network may be down.

agent-stale.title: Agent feed paused
agent-stale.body: No fresh heartbeat for {ago}. The values shown are the last the agent reported and resume on the next update.

no-camera.title: No camera detected
no-camera.body: This agent reports no camera on its CSI or USB ports. Plug a camera in and rescan, or check the camera service on the board.

air-side.title: No camera — air-side
air-side.body: The agent's video pipeline reports no primary camera right now. Reseat or power-cycle the USB camera on the drone.

no-npu.title: No AI accelerator found
no-npu.body: On-device detection needs an NPU-class compute module. This board reports none, so the vision engine stays off.

no-radio.title: No long range radio
no-radio.body: This agent has no supported WFB-ng adapter. Add one to unlock extended range datalinks and live signal stats.

no-peripherals.title: No peripherals found
no-peripherals.body: The scan checked USB, CSI and I2C and found nothing attached. Connect a sensor, camera or modem and scan again.

no-flights.title: No flights recorded yet
no-flights.body: Flight logs appear here after the first armed flight or autonomous mission with this drone.

loading.title: Connecting to the agent
loading.body: Waiting for the first heartbeat. This usually takes a few seconds. If nothing arrives, confirm the agent is powered and on the network.

stalePaused.tooltip: Paused at last reading {ago}. Values resume when the heartbeat returns.

linkUp.disambiguation: Connect flight controller talks to the autopilot directly over USB or your network. Pair a companion computer adds a board running the ADOS agent for video, scripts and 4G.

blackbox.title: ADOS Black Box
blackbox.exportUnavailable: Export is not available on this agent
blackbox.unavailable: No durable store on this agent
blackbox.cloudUnavailable: Connect on the local network to read the durable store
blackbox.unavailableHint: The durable log and telemetry store is read over the local network. Older agents fall back to the live log view.
blackbox.pushStarted: Window exported to the cloud account
blackbox.pushAlreadyStored: Window already stored in the cloud account
blackbox.pushNeedsPairing: Pair this drone to a cloud account to push
blackbox.pushNeedsLocal: Connect on the local network to push the durable store
blackbox.pushedDownloadFailed: Could not get a download link

operatingRegion.title: Operating region
operatingRegion.unrestrictedCaveat: No operating region is pinned. The radio transmits on the configured channel without a verified regulatory domain. The operator is responsible for legal RF compliance in their jurisdiction.
operatingRegion.optionUnrestrictedHint: Radio works on the configured channel without a pinned region. Operator responsible for local RF compliance.
operatingRegion.optionOtherHint: Enter a two-letter ISO country code.
operatingRegion.otherInvalid: Enter a valid two-letter ISO country code.
operatingRegion.applied: Operating region updated. The radio re-reads it on restart.
operatingRegion.applyFailed: Could not update the operating region.
operatingRegion.applyHintUnrestricted: The radio transmits on the configured channel without a pinned region. The agent re-reads this on its next restart.
operatingRegion.applyHintRegion: Pinning a region enables the strict regulatory gate and the region’s legal power limit. The agent re-reads this on its next restart.
operatingRegion.readOnlyCloud: Read-only over the cloud relay. Connect to this node over the LAN to change its operating region.
operatingRegion.readOnlyNoAgent: Connect to this node to change its operating region.
